prose:
**Aniline frontier-orbital result** — Property: HOMO–LUMO gap from a DFT frontier-orbitals single-point calculation. Level of theory: tier `standard` (**B3LYP/def2-TZVP**) using PySCF (`program`: `pyscf`; version not returned). Geometry: user-supplied fixed XYZ geometry at `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/aniline/aniline.xyz` (14 atoms); the geometry was not optimized in this task. Environment: **gas phase** (no solvent). Charge 0, multiplicity 1. SCF converged in 9 cycles. Engine integrity verdict: `trustworthy: true`. No warnings were reported in the returned result.

Headline result: HOMO–LUMO gap = **5.3048177 eV**.

Additional returned orbital values: HOMO = -5.553772 eV; LUMO = -0.2489544 eV. Total electronic energy = -287.71578 Hartree (-7829.1453 eV).

Exact chemkit invocation returned by the engine: `/home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py frontier /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/aniline/aniline.xyz --method dft --tier standard --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --charge 0 --mult 1 --stdout path --out /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpq69rn4je/live.json`.

Output JSON path from the invocation: `/home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpq69rn4je/live.json`. The chemkit result did not return a live `.out` log path, so I cannot truthfully name one beyond the returned artifacts.
